calendar.matters:
ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jan-03-2018 Off Calendar

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Nov-09-2017 Continued To Jan-23-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-25-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Xin Yu Wu, In Pro Per (510-534-6250) appears as self-represented plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Dale N. Chen of the law offices of Dale Nathan Chen, (408-562-1000) appears as self-represented counsel defendant/demurring party

No other appearance is made in this matter

Cantonese interpreter, Benjamin Giang appears to render services, but is sent away as plaintiff was not present. Plaintiff appears late and the matter is re-called for hearing. United Language Group interpreter, Li Huang, I.D. 7239 interprets English/Cantonese for the plaintiff. Certified Interpreter, Carmen Li, oath onfile, also interprets English/Cantonese for the plaintiff.

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling and reads the ruling in open court with both sides present.

DEFENDANT DALE CHEN'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT, as to all causes of action in the first amended complaint is SUSTAINED without leave to amend as to all causes of action. Plaintiffs Jing Ming Wu and Xin Yu Wu's request for a further continuance of the hearing on this demurrer is DENIED. At the May 23, 2017 hearing where plaintiffs were given four months to locate an attorney, the court stated that there will be no more continuances of this hearing. Even if the court would entertain a request for a further continuance, plaintiffs have not stated what efforts they have made to locate an attorney nor have they made any showing that they can allege a viable claim against Mr. Chen. Based on the record on this demurrer, it does not appear reasonably possible that the plaintiffs can allege facts that support a viable claim against Mr. Chen. Written order is signed and filed in open court. The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] judgment that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim and that complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter, CSR No. 6576, email: rhunter@sftc.org. (D302)

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-25-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Xin Yu Wu, In Pro Per (510-534-6250) appears as self-represented plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Dale N. Chen of the law offices of Dale Nathan Chen, (408-562-1000) appears as self-represented counsel defendant/demurring party

No other appearance is made in this matter

Cantonese interpreter, Benjamin Giang appears to render services, but is sent away as plaintiff was not present. Plaintiff appears late and the matter is re-called for hearing. United Language Group interpreter, Li Huang, I.D. 7239 interprets English/Cantonese for the plaintiff. Certified Interpreter, Carmen Li, oath onfile, also interprets English/Cantonese for the plaintiff.

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling and reads the ruling in open court with both sides present.

DEFENDANT DALE CHEN'S MOTION TO STRIKE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT is OFF CALENDAR in light of the ruling on Mr. Chen's demurrer. A written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter, CSR No. 6576, email: rhunter@sftc.org. (D302)

MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - JING MING WU, PLAINTIFF APPEARING IN PRO PER, TEL. NO. 1-510-534-6250;
CATHERINE HONG, CANTONESE INTERPRETER ALSO PRESENT IN COURT;
DALE N. CHEN, TEL. 1-408-562-1000, DEFENDANT APPEARING IN PRO PER.

RULING - THE MATTER WAS DISCUSSED. DEFENDANT DALE CHEN'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is continued to September 25, 2017 in order to give Ms. Wu an opportunity to hire an attorney. This will be the last continuance to be allowed by the court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR # 8189 =302/HK

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-24-2017 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Jing Ming Wu (510.590.0501) appears In Propria Persona as plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Dale Nathan Chen of the law offices of Dale Nathan Chen (408.562.1000) appears as self-represented defendant/moving party

Certified Cantonese Interpreter Anthony Tam, interprets English/Cantonese for the plaintiff. The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT DALE CHEN'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT filed by plaintiff Jing Ming Wu is sustained with twenty days leave to amend. The complaint is uncertain and lacks ultimate facts to show that plaintiff has any valid claim against Mr. Chen. Because of the sparsity of the allegations in the complaint, the court is unable to determine if either collateral estoppel or res judicata apply to bar any or all of the claims that plaintiff seeks to allege. Plaintiff is given leave to allege sufficient facts to support one or more valid claims against Mr. Chen that are not based on the same primary right as any of plaintiff's prior lawsuits against Mr. Chen. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Not reported. (D302)
